State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

November 2, 1978

78-11-110

Judith A. Ennis
1779 Virazon Drive
La Habra, California 90631

Dear Mrs. Ennis:

Thank you for your letter of October 22, 1978, in which you ask about the provisions of the Political Reform Act which require identification of campaign literature.

Government Code Section 84305 requires any mass mailing to have on its envelope and on one of its inserts the name and address of the person responsible for the mailing. Because the literature must be mailed, the material enclosed in your letter may not come within the provisions of the Act since it apparently was distributed door to door.

Elections Code Section 29410 has a similar provision which applies to all literature. However, this section is not interpreted nor enforced by our agency. The Elections Code provisions are interpreted by the Secretary of State and the Attorney General and by your local district attorney.

The Committee to Elect Fred Luevano is not registered with the Secretary of State. However, it does not have to register unless it has raised $500 or more.

Please feel free to call on me if you have any further questions.

Sincerely,

Robert M. Stern
General Counsel

RMS:nc

26

1779 Virazon Drive
La Habra, California 90631
October 22, 1978


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Sir:

The cover sheet "incorporation is not free" was taped to the door
of my mailbox in La Habra Heights on Saturday, October 21, 1978;
the letter and two budget sheets were lying nearby. I saw many
identical mailers taped to boxes or blowing about; one for each
home amounts to 1,600 packets.

I understand that according to fair campaign practice laws, the
name of the responsible committee must appear on each sheet of
campaign literature. This has not been done on this literature.
So, I protest this as illegal.

Is there a registered Committee to Elect Fred Luevano and has he
filed an expense statement? Is he running an illegal campaign,
as well?

This is the campaign for incorporation of La Habra Heights,
proposition S.S. on the November ballot.

Sincerely,

Mrs. George Ennis
(Judith A. Ennis)

"income sources will provide more than sufficient revenue"

"You will save money by voting for incorporation"

"at no additional cost to you"


Incorporation is not free!


Look inside for the facts —>

Dear Members of the La Habra Heights Community:

Allow me to take a few moments of your time to introduce myself and members of the committee of Luevano for Council.

My name is Fred Luevano, Jr. Currently I am employed by the City of Anaheim as computer operations manager where I manage a multi-agency service center which provides Data Processing services to eleven cities. As computer operations manager, I represent the City of Anaheim in Board of Directors meetings which include City Council members, City managers, and Finance Directors from various cities. I received a Bachelor of Arts Degree in Public Management from the University of Redlands. My wife, Lupe, my children, Michael and James, and I are newcomers to the community of La Habra Heights. We completed our home in February of this year and look forward to enjoying the rural environment of La Habra Heights.

As your Councilman in the proposed city of La Habra Heights, my primary objectives are:

1. to monitor revenues and expenditures to assure proper checks and balances of taxpayer funds.

2. to oppose any measure that would burden the taxpayers of this community.

3. to support the existing General Plan and to maintain a rural environment.

4. to minimize the level of risk to the community by supporting adequate police and fire protection.

The Luevano for Council committee is comprised of Mrs. Jean Flath, treasurer, Annette Taylor, Barbara Taylor and Pat Messinger. The members of this committee and myself fully support local control of La Habra Heights, the General Plan, the Volunteer Fire Department and the many advantages of incorporation. We, however, believe that the taxpayers of this community should be aware that there are potential risks to incorporation. For these reasons we have prepared a cost analysis of the LAFCD reports, the HAYS study, the Proponents for Incorporation budget, a revised Incorporation Budget based on approximate actual population and the City of Walnut, a contract city. The majority of the revenues would be derived as a result of population and we feel that the revenues derived from actual population would be closer to reality than would the 7,716 population revenue figures (registered voters X 3) used by LAFCD, HAYES, and Proponents for Incorporation.

We have chosen the City of Walnut for comparison purposes because they have a contract city, nearly the same area in size and a population slightly higher than ours. Hidden Hills (1.36 square miles and population of 1,560) and Bradbury (1.99 square miles and 880 population) as stated in the LAFCD report do not compare to our community.

I wish to thank the following people for the long hours contributed towards making this analysis possible and for the information they have contributed. Mr. El Hathaway of the Proponents for Incorporation, Mr. Pete Kraeger of CCIN, and the members of the Luevano for Council committee.

We hope this analysis will assist you in your decision making process and on November 7, 1978 please VOTE FOR THE CANDIDATE THAT WILL KEEP YOU INFORMED AND ACT IN YOUR BEHALF. Thank you!

[Signature]

1. actual Golf Club sales tax = $5,881.
2. it is $25.00 per license now.
3. the Water Co. records showed 18 new home meter turn ons for the
   first 6 months of this year.  El Hathaway states in the Application
   for Incorporation that about 20 new homes would be built every
   year, but now believes that 80 to 100 new homes will be built
   each year.

2. Capt. Rockett of LA Co. said "65,000 isn't even in the ball park" for adequate protection if hired by law.
3. Knowledgeable source states This is quite low - doesn't take all costs into consideration.
